note:
全新的工作流程可用于编辑网格，新的物理模拟方法，更快的Cycles渲染，与Eevee更好的合成等等。 Blender 2.92标志着令人难以置信的事情的开始。  新的“几何节点”编辑器为使用基于节点的系统创建和操纵网格打开了大门。  创建您自己的自定义修饰符！ 从修改器堆栈中的节点公开属性，以提供易于使用的界面。  第一次迭代着重于对象的分散和实例化，为即将发布的版本中更高级的工作流奠定了基础。  在极限表面上涂抹多分辨率位移，类似于颜色和拓扑幻灯片的涂抹方式。  可以在同一区域上多次使用拖影，而不会在拓扑结构中生成伪影。 在视频中，使用了扩展涂片模式。  当用于薄网格物体时，从物体的一侧抓取不会影响另一侧的形状。 
该模式使用开尔文（Kelvinlet）使网格变形，而不是直接在画笔半径内部应用位移，这对于样式化的形状草绘非常有用。  Mesh Fairing使您可以通过完全平滑Face Set内部的区域，从视觉上删除网格的一部分。  以前，较长的行程会被切断。 使用新算法，可以正确地“拉伸”笔触以完全适合极端形状。  通过使用稀疏的NanoVDB网格，体积渲染显着提高了内存效率。 阅读更多。  OptiX现在支持混合渲染，以结合您的CPU和GPU设备的功能。  *确保使用最新的驱动程序（450或更高版本）来利用所有功能和优化。  APIC产生了非常有力的但也更稳定的模拟。 液体中的涡流将比FLIP保留得更好。 
左侧是曝光节点的结果，右侧是原始图像。
note_en:
A completely new workflow for editing meshes, new physics simulation methods, faster Cycles rendering, better compositing with Eevee, and so much more. Blender 2.92 marks the beginning of something incredible.
 The new Geometry Nodes editor opens the door for creating and manipulating meshes using a node-based system.
 Create your own custom modifier! Expose properties from the nodes in the modifier stack for an easy-to-use interface.
 This first iteration focuses on object scattering and instancing, laying the groundwork for more advanced workflows in the upcoming releases.
  Smear multires displacement over the limit surface similar to how smearing for colors and topology slide works.
    Smearing can be used multiple times over the same area without generating artifacts in the topology. In the video the Expand smear mode is used.
 When used on thin meshes, grabbing from one side of the object won’t affecting the shape on the other side.
 This mode deforms the mesh using a kelvinlet instead of applying the displacement directly inside the brush radius, which is great for stylized shapes sketching.
 Mesh Fairing allows you to visually remove parts of your mesh by completely smoothing the area inside a Face Set.
 Previously, the longer stroke would be cut off. With the new algorithm, the strokes are properly “stretched” to fit the extreme shapes exactly.
    Volume rendering is significantly more memory efficient, by using a sparse NanoVDB grid.  Read more.
 OptiX now supports  hybrid rendering to combine the power of your CPU and GPU devices.
  * Make sure to use the latest drivers (450 or newer) to make use of all the features and optimizations.
 APIC produces a very energetic but also more stable simulation. Vortices within the liquid will be preserved better than with FLIP.
  On the left side the result of the exposure node, on the right the raw image.

note_en:
You'll notice that execution starts off by treating the Windows PEheader as though it were code. For example, the ASCII string  "MZqFpD"decodes as  pop %r10 ; jno 0x4a ; jo 0x4a and the string "\177ELF" decodes as  jg 0x47. It then hopsthrough a mov statement which tells us the program is being run fromuserspace rather than being booted, and then hops to the entrypoint. Magic numbers are then  easilyunpacked for the host operating system using decentralized sectionsand the GNU Assembler  .sleb128 directive. Low entropy datalike UNICODE bit lookup tables will generally be decoded using eithera  103byte LZ4 decompressor ora  17byte run-length decoder, and runtime code morphing can easily bedone usingIntel's  3kbx86 decoder. Please note that this emulator isn't a requirement. αcτµαlly pδrταblεεxεcµταblεs work fine if you just run them on the shell, the NT commandprompt, or boot them from the BIOS. This isn't a JVM. You only use theemulator if you need it. For example, it's helpful to be able to havecool visualizations of how program execution impacts memory. It'll be nice to know that any normal PC program we write will "justwork" on Raspberry Pi and Apple ARM. All we have to do embed an ARMbuild of the emulator above within our x86 executables, and have themmorph and re-exec appropriately, similar to how Cosmopolitan is alreadydoing doing with qemu-x86_64, except that this wouldn't need to beinstalled beforehand. The tradeoff is that, if we do this, binaries willonly be 10x smaller than Go's Hello World, instead of 100x smaller. Theother tradeoff is the GCC Runtime Exception forbids code morphing, but Ialready took care of that for you, by rewriting the GNU runtimes. The most compelling use case for making x86-64-linux-gnu as tiny aspossible, with the availability of full emulation, is that it enablesnormal simple native programs to run everywhere including web browsersby default. Many of the solutions built in this area tend to focus toomuch on the interfaces that haven't achieved consensus, like GUIs andthreads, otherwise they'll just emulate the entire operating system,like Docker or Fabrice Bellard running Windows in browsers. I think weneed compatibility glue that just runs programs, ignores the systems,and treats x86_64-linux-gnu as a canonical software encoding.   One of the reasons why I love working with a lot of these old unsexytechnologies, is that I want any software work I'm involved in to standthe test of time with minimal toil. Similar to how the Super Mario BrosROM has managed to survive all these years without needing a GitHubissue tracker. I believe the best chance we have of doing that, is by gluing togetherthe binary interfaces that've already achieved a decades-long consensus,and ignoring the APIs. For example, here are the magicnumbers used by Mac, Linux, BSD, and Windows distros. They're worthseeing at least once in your life, since these numbers underpin theinternals of nearly all the computers, servers, and phones you've used. If we focus on the subset of numbers all systems share in common, andcompare it to their common ancestor, Bell System Five, we can see thatfew things about systems engineering have changed in the last 40 yearsat the binary level. Magnums are boring. Platforms can't break themwithout breaking themselves. Few people have proposed visions over theyears on why UNIX numerology needs to change. download                 emulator.com (280k PE+ELF+MachO+ZIP+SH)   tinyemu.com (188k PE+ELF+MachO+ZIP+SH) source code   ape.S   ape.lds   blinkenlights.c   x86ild.greg.c   syscalls.sh   consts.sh   programs  life.com (12kb ape  symbols)    sha256.elf (3kb x86_64-linux-gnu)    hello.bin (55b x86_64-linux-gnu) example   bash hello.com # runs it natively./hello.com # runs it natively./tinyemu.com hello.com # just runs program./emulator.com -t life.com # show debugger guiecho hello | ./emulator.com sha256.elf

note:
内华达州州长史蒂夫·西索拉克（Steve Sisolak）今天将宣布法律，该法律将允许主要的技术公司有效地组建技术政府。
 州长西索拉克（Sisolak）于1月19日在州内致辞中首次提到了在内华达州建立“创新区”的提议。“创造突破性技术的新公司可以来到内华达州发展自己的产业。这将无需减税或公共融资。”
 根据《拉斯维加斯评论报》获得的法案草案，该立法不会提供子公司或公共资金，但将授权大型技术公司在内华达州组建独立的技术政府。拉斯维加斯评论杂志报道：“ [他们]将具有与郡县相同的权力，包括征收税款，组建学区和司法法院以及提供政府服务的能力。”
   在1月份的讲话中，西索拉克州长特别提到了Blockchains，LLC公司，该公司已经致力于在该州创建一个“完全依靠区块链技术运行”的技术政府。州长西索拉克（Sisolak）表示，此举将使内华达州“成为新兴产业的中心，并创造与之相关的高薪工作和收入。”
 用最简单的术语来说，区块链是一种数据库系统，它以分组形式存储计算信息，称为“块”。尽管并非完全不可改变，但区块链技术在设计上被认为是安全的，因为各方之间的数据交易被有效且以开放，可验证且永久的方式记录。金融服务，尤其是加密货币，广泛使用区块链技术。
 Gov. Sisolak，Blockchains，LLC明确提到的公司由消费者保护律师和加密货币百万富翁Jeffrey Berns于2014年创立。
 根据他们的网站Blockchains，LLC目前在内华达州斯托里县拥有超过67,000英亩的土地。该公司表示，他们的目标是将这片土地转变为“该国企业和居民最先进的'高科技'社区和社会”。 
Blockchains称其为“一种新的生活方式”，他们“相信我们的产品套件的集成可以实现智能社区，数字经济和互联社会的全部潜力。”
 “区块链旨在展示如何与世界变化的技术一起发展商业发展，住宅生活和商业。为此，我们必须从头开始，否则，我们只是试图将智能技术插入并非“智能”的设备中。阅读公司的“发展之路”计划。
 根据国家记录，2018年，区块链以1.7亿美元的价格在塔霍里诺工业中心购买了67125英亩无人居住的土地。当时，该公司的网站上没有列出任何管理人员，电话号码或软件功能的明确说明，导致一些当地媒体将其标记为“神秘”。
 自从争夺土地以来，区块链一直在努力游说以通过立法，以使该公司组建自己的技术政府。
 拉斯维加斯评论期刊报道Blockchains，LLC向政治行动委员会Home Means Nevada捐赠了50,000美元，该委员会管理了Sisolak于2019年1月上任的过渡。 2018。
 记录还显示，该公司的所有者杰弗里·伯恩斯（Jeffrey Berns）于2019年亲自向内华达州民主党捐款5万美元，并向双方各州议员分别捐款1,000至5,000美元。
 一周多以前，The Debrief联系了Blockchains，LLC进行评论;但是，该公司未响应此请求。 
在向The Debrief发出的电子邮件中，州长办公室传播总监Meghin Delaney表示，Sisolack州长将在2月26日星期五下午4:30（美国东部时间）举行的虚拟新闻发布会上正式宣布拟议的``创新区''立法。 ）。
 与州长Sisolack一起参加今天的会议的是州长经济发展办公室执行主任迈克尔·布朗（Michael Brown）和应用分析（Applied Analysis）首席分析师杰里米·阿奎罗（Jeremy Aguero）。
 根据《拉斯维加斯评论杂志》的说法，提案中的措辞草案说，传统的地方政府模式“不足以单独提供足够的灵活性和资源，有利于使国家成为吸引和保留新形式和新类型的企业并促进发展的领导者。新兴技术和创新产业的经济发展。”
 据报道，该提案草案还说，需要建立技术政府或“地方政府的替代形式”，以帮助该州的经济发展。
 总督经济发展办公室计划处理创新区的申请，这些创新区将仅限于特定的“创新技术”，包括“区块链，自主技术，物联网，人工智能，无线技术，生物识别技术和可再生资源”技术。”
 总督的经济发展办公室将《汇报》中有关支持技术政府的立法问题转交给总督的通讯人员。由于尚未正式提出该法案，因此获得通过。
 提案草案对公司创建自己的技术政府提出了要求，包括拥有至少50,000英亩未开发和无人居住土地的申请人，这些政府都在一个县内，但与任何城市，城镇或税收增加区分开。 
大型科技公司还必须至少投资2.5亿美元用于初期开发，并计划在未来十年内在该地区额外投资10亿美元。
 大型科技公司将充当其自己的独立政府机构，由三名成员组成的监事会，其职权与县专员委员会相同。该法案的拟议草案建议，技术公司对于谁将担任技术政府董事会拥有重大发言权。
 虽然提案草案提供了对该概念背后的某些细节的洞察力，但在正式提出该法案时，某些方面可能会发生变化。
     除了受到诸如区块链之类公司的游说之外，提议允许技术政府的主要动机是希望此举将极大地促进内华达州陷入困境的经济。
 内华达州旅游业间接或直接占该州经济的近25％，内华达州已因COVID-19大流行而遭受重创。大流行之前，拉斯维加斯是美国第二大市政旅游业，为内华达州创造了超过190亿美元的GDP。根据内华达州度假协会的统计，旅游和休闲服务业占内华达州每三份工作中的一项。
 布鲁金斯学会（Brookings Institute）发现内华达州的两个城市，拉斯维加斯和里诺（Reno），是大流行病打击最严重的三大都会经济体之一。 2020年12月，美国劳工统计局报告，内华达州的失业率为9.2％，是美国第二高的失业率。倒数第二位的夏威夷，仅比内华达州（9.3％）低十分之一。
 相反，西雅图和旧金山等专注于技术的大都市枢纽则从COVID-19蓬勃发展并从中受益。从2019年11月到2020年11月，非商店零售商（即在线购物）的销售额增长了15％。总部位于西雅图的在线零售巨头亚马逊的员工人数几乎翻了一番，在2020年增加了40万个工作岗位。社交媒体巨头Facebook宣布了计划2020年4月再雇用10,000名工人。 
格言“最安全的工作是联邦政府的工作”在COVID-19大流行期间一直存在，美国政府在2020年增加了50,000多个工作。美国大都会华盛顿特区的政府就业率去年增长了2％以上。
 通过允许主要的技术公司组建技术政府，内华达州将试图通过诱使那些在经济不景气的时期内受益最多的行业组合来捕获瓶中的闪电。
 尽管经济利益似乎很诱人，但几乎可以肯定的是，公众对大技术被允许组建自己的主权治理的想法感到担忧。
 在2018年的一篇文章中，技术分析师杰米·巴特利特（Jamie Bartlett）警告说，技术垄断对民主国家构成了真正的威胁。巴特利特写道：“科技只是非常富有的人使用经过考验的技术来购买政治影响力，垄断行为和避免监管的最新手段，”
 过去两年来，人们对大型高科技公司所拥有的力量的恐惧才有所增加。公众和政界人士都对大型科技公司成为文化霸权，主导公众思想和信念的潜力表示担忧。
 如果拟议的立法获得通过，允许大技术组建技术政府的最终影响尚无定论。
 在区块链计划建立“更好的生活方式”的地区，斯托伊县专员兰斯·吉尔曼在接受《拉斯维加斯日报评论》采访时对拟议的立法有些犹豫。 “ [这将对斯托里县产生影响，而对于这将是正面还是负面的判断尚无定论。” 
在Twitter或Facebook上加入我们，参与讨论并分享您对允许成立Techno-Governments的Big Tech的想法。 您还可以在Flipboard，Instagram上关注The Debrief上的所有最新新闻和令人兴奋的功能内容，不要忘记订阅The Debrief YouTube频道并查看The Official Debrief播客。
note_en:
Nevada Governor Steve Sisolak will be announcing legislation today that will allow major technology companies to effectively form techno-governments.
 Gov. Sisolak first mentioned the proposal of creating “Innovation Zones” in Nevada during his   State-of-the-State address  on January 19. “New companies creating groundbreaking technologies can come to Nevada to develop their industries. This will be done without tax abatements or public financing.”
 While the legislation wouldn’t provide subsidiaries or public funding, according to a draft of the Bill obtained by the   Las Vegas Review-Journal , major technology firms would be granted authority to form their independent techno-governments within Nevada. “[They] would carry the same authority as a county, including the ability to impose taxes, form school districts and justice courts and provide government services, to name a few duties,” Las Vegas Review-Journal reports.
   During his remarks in January, Gov. Sisolak specifically mentioned the company Blockchains, LLC as already being committed to creating a techno-government in the state that would “fully run on blockchain technology.” Gov. Sisolak said the move would make Nevada “the epicenter of this emerging industry and creating the high paying jobs and revenue that go with it.”
 In the simplest terms, blockchain is a type of database system that stores computational information in groups, known as “blocks.” Though not entirely unalterable, blockchain technology is considered secure by design because data transactions between parties are recorded efficiently and in an open, verifiable, and permanent manner. Financial services, particularly cryptocurrencies, widely use blockchain technology.
 The company mentioned explicitly by Gov. Sisolak, Blockchains, LLC, was founded in 2014 by consumer protection attorney and cryptocurrency millionaire  Jeffrey Berns.
 According to their   website , Blockchains, LLC currently owns over 67,000 acres in Storey County, Nevada. The company says they aim to convert this land into “the most advanced ‘high-tech’ community and society for business and residents in the country.”
 Calling it “a new way to live,” Blockchains says they “believe the integration of our product suite is where the full potential of a smart community, digital economy, and connected society can be realized.”
 “Blockchains aims to showcase how business development, residential living, and commerce can flourish alongside world-changing technologies. To do that, we have to start with a blank slate – otherwise, we’d merely be trying to insert smart technologies into devices that aren’t, well, ‘smart,'” reads the company’s “  Road to Development ” plan.
 According to state records, in 2018, Blockchains purchased 67,125 acres of uninhabited land at the Tahoe Reno Industrial Center for $170 million. At the time, the company’s website did not list any executive, a phone number, or a clear explanation of what it’s software did, leading some local media outlets to label the company as “mysterious.”
 Since the land grab, Blockchains has been lobbying hard to get legislation passed that would allow the company to form its own techno-government.
 Las Vegas Review-Journal reports Blockchains, LLC gave $50,000 to a political action committee, Home Means Nevada, which managed Sisolak’s transition into office in January 2019. Campaign   finance records  also show the company donated $10,000 to Sisolak and his Republican opponent Adam Laxalt’s campaign in 2018.
 Records also show the company’s owner, Jeffrey Berns, personally gave $50,000 to the Nevada Democratic Party in 2019 and various donations ranging from $1,000 to $5,000 to various state lawmakers from both parties.
 Over a week ago,   The Debrief  reached out to Blockchains, LLC for comment; however, the company did not respond to this request.
    In an email to   The Debrief , Communications Director for the Governor’s office, Meghin Delaney, said Gov. Sisolack will formally unveil the proposed “Innovation Zones” legislation during a virtual press conference today, Friday, February 26, at 4:30 pm (EST).
 Joining Gov. Sisolack at today’s conference will be Michael Brown, Executive Director of the Governor’s Office of Economic Development, and Jeremy Aguero, principal analyst at Applied Analysis.
 According to the   Las Vegas Review-Journal , the draft language in the proposal said the traditional local government model was “inadequate alone to provide the flexibility and resources conducive to making the State a leader in attracting and retaining new forms and types of businesses and fostering economic development in emerging technologies and innovative industries.”
 The draft proposal also reportedly said the creation of techno-governments or “alternative forms of local government” were needed to aid economic development within the state.
 The Governor’s Office of Economic Development is slated to handle applications for the Innovation Zones, which would be limited to specific “innovative technologies,” including “blockchain, autonomous technology, the internet of things, artificial intelligence, wireless technology, biometrics, and renewable resource technology.”
 The Governor’s Office of Economic Development redirected  The Debrief’s questions on the legislation backing techno-governments to the Governor’s communications staff; since the Bill had not been formally proposed, much less passed.
 The draft proposal laid out the requirements for companies to create their own techno-government, including an applicant owning at least 50,000 acres of undeveloped and uninhabited land, all within a single county but separate from any city, town or tax increment area.
 Big Tech companies would also have to pledge at least $250 million toward initial development, with a plan to invest an additional $1 billion over ten years in the zone.
 Major technology companies would function as their own independent governmental body, with a three-member board of supervisors that would carry the same authority as a board of county commissioners. The Bill’s proposed draft suggests technology companies would have a significant say over who would sit on techno-government’s board.
 While the draft proposal provides insight into some of the details behind the concept, some aspects could be changed when the Bill is formally introduced.
     Aside from lobbying by companies such as Blockchains, the primary motivation for proposing the allowance of techno-governments would be the hope that the move would significantly boost Nevada’s beleaguered economy.
 With tourism indirectly or directly accounting for   almost 25%  of the state’s economy, Nevada has been devastated by the COVID-19 pandemic. Las Vegas, the United States’ second-largest municipal tourism industry, accounted for over $19 billion of Nevada’s GDP before the pandemic. According to the   Nevada Resort Association , tourism and leisure services account for one out of every three jobs in Nevada.
 The   Brookings Institute  found two Nevada cities, Las Vegas and Reno, were among the top three metro economies hardest-hit by the pandemic. In December 2020, the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics reported Nevada’s unemployment rate of 9.2% was the second-highest in the United States. Hawaii, which came in dead last, was only a tenth of a percentage point below Nevada at 9.3%.
 Conversely, metropolitan hubs specializing in technology, such as Seattle and San Francisco, have thrived and benefited from COVID-19. Sales at non-store retailers (i.e., online shopping) increased by 15% from November 2019 to November 2020. Online retail giant Amazon, headquartered in Seattle,   nearly doubled its workforce , adding 400,000 jobs in 2020. Social media monolith Facebook   announced  plans to hire an additional 10,000 workers in April 2020.
 The adage that “the most secure job is a federal government job” has held during the COVID-19 pandemic, and the U.S. Government grew by over 50,000 jobs in 2020. The metropolitan seat of America’s power, Washington D.C., saw a government employment rate increase over 2% last year.
 By allowing major technology corporations to form their techno-governments, Nevada will be trying to capture lightning in a bottle by enticing a mix of the most benefited industries during these economically downtrodden times.
 While economic benefits seem enticing, almost assuredly, there will be some public concerns over the idea of Big Tech being allowed to form its own sovereign governance.
 In a   2018 essay , technology analyst Jamie Bartlett warned technology monopolies posed a real threat to democracies. “Tech is just the latest vehicle for very rich people to use well-tested techniques of buying political influence, monopolistic behavior and avoiding regulation,” wrote Bartlett.
 Fear over the power Big Tech companies wield has only increased in the past two years. The public and politicians alike have expressed concerns over Big Tech companies’ potential to be a cultural hegemony, dominating public ideas and beliefs.
 The jury is still out as to the ultimate impact of allowing Big Tech to form techno-governments, provided that the proposed legislation passes.
 In the area Blockchains plans to build their “better way to live,” Storey County Commissioner Lance Gilman was a little hesitant toward the proposed legislation during an interview with the Las Vegas Journal Review. “[It’s] going to have an impact on Storey County, and the jury is still out on whether that will be positive or negative.”
 Join us on   Twitter  or   Facebook  to weigh in and share your thoughts on Big Tech being allowed to form Techno-Governments. You can also follow all the latest  news and exciting feature content from The Debrief on   Flipboard ,   Instagram , and don’t forget to subscribe to   The Debrief YouTube Channel  and check out   The Official Debrief Podcast .
title: 允许大技术组成“技术政府”的法案今天宣布
title_en: Bill Allowing Big Tech to Form “Techno-Governments” to Be Announced Today

高管们在周四的一次内部会议上表示，Facebook正在讨论将面部识别技术纳入其即将推出的智能眼镜产品中，并一直在权衡这一有争议技术的法律含义。
 在预定的全公司会议上，Facebook增强和虚拟现实副总裁安德鲁·博斯沃思（Andrew Bosworth）告诉员工，该公司目前正在评估它是否具有在据称将于今年晚些时候推出的设备上提供面部识别的合法能力。他说，尚未做出任何决定，他指出，现行的州法律可能使Facebook无法向人们提供基于其面部图片搜索他人的能力。
 博斯沃思在回答员工关于员工是否人际关系的问题时说：“人脸识别……可能是最棘手的问题，在这种情况下好处如此明显，而风险却如此清晰，我们不知道该在哪里平衡。”当智能眼镜成为一种流行的技术时，将能够“将他们的脸标记为不可搜索”。这位不愿透露姓名的工人特别强调了对包括“潜行者”在内的“现实世界中的危害”的担忧。
 故事发表后，博斯沃思（Bosworth）在推特上写道，我们一直在努力开发[增强现实]眼镜，目前仍处于早期阶段。
 面部识别是一个极具争议性的话题，这是有充分理由的，我在讲的是我们将如何就优缺点进行公开讨论，＆＃34;他说。
 自去年以来，Facebook就一直在吹捧智能眼镜产品，称它将在2021年“迟于上市”。与雷朋及其母公司Luxottica Group合作，可穿戴设备有望与竞争对手的产品抗衡。 Snapchat和亚马逊，提供增强现实体验，将数字功能叠加在人们的现实世界视角上。
 博斯沃斯（Bosworth）对Facebook面部识别设备的评论之时正值有关该技术的激烈辩论和审查之时，该技术已由联邦机构，警察部门甚至私人公司部署，通常以安全和保障的名义进行。评论家和学者谴责缺乏对该技术的联邦法规，他们说这可能损害个人隐私。包括旧金山和波士顿在内的一些城市已经禁止将其用于政府用途。 
Facebook是用户上传照片的最大存储库之一，它对人脸识别采取了谨慎的态度。 2015年，该公司部署了一个名为DeepFace的系统，此系统自那时以来一直用于增强功能，例如照片标签建议，以识别照片中的人物。与将面部识别工具出售给其他公司和公共实体的亚马逊不同，Facebook并未销售DeepFace或其其他任何面部识别技术以供其自己的社交网络使用。
 博斯沃思在周四的讲话中表示，他一直在与CEO马克·扎克伯格（Mark Zuckerberg）讨论面部识别，今天晚些时候，他将负责智能眼镜产品的隐私审查。去年年底，该副总裁还在一份内部备忘录中指出，该公司应“根据隐私来区分我们的产品。”该备忘录最初是由大技术通讯报道的。
 “我们应该成为提供隐私保护软件的无可争议的领导者，” Bosworth在12月写道。
    博斯沃思（Bosworth）表示，他周四了解面部识别对隐私的担忧，但他批评了当前的立法，包括伊利诺伊州的《生物识别信息隐私法》（BIPA），该法案禁止私人公司未经人的同意收集和存储诸如面部扫描之类的生物识别数据。他指出，该法律于2008年通过，并质疑这种过时的立法如何适用于当今的技术。
 去年，Facebook被勒令向伊利诺伊州公民支付6.5亿美元，原因是该公司违反了带有照片标签功能的BIPA。
 “真正的问题是，我们是否能够识别所有面孔，我们还是不知道。从法律上讲，如果您熟悉伊利诺伊州的BIPA，答案可能是“不”，因为人们将人脸识别定为非法。” Bosworth周四对员工说。 “没关系。作为一个社会，我们可以做到这一点，而没有它，这个产品将生存和繁荣。不过，我确实认为有些机会错失了。”
 在解释面部识别的可能用例时，博斯沃思说，Facebook的智能眼镜可以帮助某人在宴会上认出某人，如果他们不记得自己的名字或面部盲。 
在谈话中，Facebook首席多样性官Maxine Williams指出，Facebook可能需要发展自己的面部识别原则，而在目前尚无法律管辖该技术的地方，这些原则将占据上风。她补充说：“仅仅因为您可以[制造某些东西]，并不意味着您会这么做。”她指出，该公司将不得不考虑该产品潜在的危害和歧视。
 随着Facebook探索在将来的产品中添加面部识别的可能性，它一直在与一家公司刮擦照片和数据以构建自己的面部识别工具。 Clearview AI已向数百个警察局，联邦机构和私人公司提供了其软件，其部分训练了从Facebook和Instagram拍摄的照片的面部识别工具。
 尽管Facebook先前曾表示已因违反其服务条款而向Clearview发送了终止通知，但该公司尚未对这家总部位于纽约的初创公司采取法律行动。 Facebook董事会成员彼得·泰尔（Peter Thiel）是Clearview的早期投资者。
note_en:
Facebook is discussing building facial recognition into its upcoming smart glasses product and has been weighing the legal implications of the controversial technology, executives said at an internal meeting Thursday.
 During a scheduled companywide meeting, Andrew Bosworth, Facebook’s vice president of augmented and virtual reality, told employees that the company is currently assessing whether or not it has the legal capacity to offer facial recognition on devices that are reportedly set to launch later this year. Nothing had been decided, he said, and he noted that current state laws may make it impossible for Facebook to offer people the ability to search for others based on pictures of their face.
 “Face recognition ... might be the thorniest issue, where the benefits are so clear, and the risks are so clear, and we don’t know where to balance those things,” Bosworth said in response to an employee question about whether people would be able to “mark their faces as unsearchable” when smart glasses become a prevalent technology. The unnamed worker specifically highlighted fears about the potential for “real-world harm,” including “stalkers.”
 Following publication of this story, Bosworth  wrote on Twitter that "we’ve been open about our efforts to build [augmented reality] glasses and are still in the early stages."
 "Face recognition is a hugely controversial topic and for good reason and I was speaking about was how we are going to have to have a very public discussion about the pros and cons," he said.
 Facebook has been touting a smart glasses product since last year, saying that it will arrive “ sooner than later” in 2021. Built in partnership with Ray-Ban and its parent company, Luxottica Group, the wearable devices are expected to rival products from competitors Snapchat and Amazon, providing an augmented reality experience that will overlay digital features on people’s real-world perspectives.
 Bosworth’s comments about a Facebook facial recognition device come at a time of heated debate and scrutiny of the technology, which has been  deployed by federal agencies, police departments, and even private companies — often in the name of safety and security. Critics and academics have  decried the lack of federal regulation over the technology, which they say could undermine individual privacy. Some cities, including San Francisco and  Boston, have already banned it from government use.
 Facebook, which has one of the largest repositories of user-uploaded photos, has taken a cautious approach to facial recognition. In 2015, it deployed a system called  DeepFace that it’s since used to power features, such as photo tag suggestions, to identify people in pictures. Unlike Amazon, which sells its facial recognition tool to other companies and public entities, Facebook has not marketed DeepFace or any of its other facial recognition technologies for use beyond its own social network.
 In his remarks Thursday, Bosworth said he has been discussing facial recognition with CEO Mark Zuckerberg and he was overseeing a privacy review for the smart glasses product later in the day. Late last year, the vice president also noted in an internal memo, first reported by  the Big Technology newsletter, that the company should “differentiate our products on the basis of privacy.”
 “We should become the undisputed leaders in providing privacy aware software,” Bosworth wrote in December.
    While Bosworth said he understood privacy concerns over facial recognition on Thursday, he criticized current legislation, including Illinois’ Biometric Information Privacy Act (BIPA), which prevents private companies from collecting and storing biometric data like face scans without people’s consent. He noted the law was passed in 2008 and questioned how such out-of-date legislation could apply to the technologies of today.
 Last year, Facebook was  ordered to pay $650 million to Illinois citizens for violating BIPA with its photo-tagging feature.
 “The real question is whether we will be able to recognize any faces at all, and we don’t know. Legally, the answer might be no, if you’re familiar with BIPA in Illinois ... people are making face recognition illegal,” Bosworth told employees Thursday. “That’s ok. We can do that as a society and this product will survive and thrive without it. I do think there are some lost opportunities, though.”
 In explaining possible use cases of facial recognition, Bosworth said that Facebook’s smart glasses may help someone recognize someone at a dinner party if they can’t remember their name or if they have  face blindness.
 During the conversation, Facebook Chief Diversity Officer Maxine Williams noted that Facebook may need to develop its own principles on facial recognition that would take precedence in places where there are currently no laws governing the technology. “Just because you can [build something,] doesn’t mean you will,” she added, noting that the company would have to consider the product’s potential for harm and discrimination.
 As Facebook explores the possibility of adding facial recognition to future products, it’s been sparring with a company that scraped its photos and data to build its own facial recognition tool. Clearview AI, which has  provided its software to hundreds of police departments, federal agencies, and private companies, trained its facial recognition tool, in part, on photos taken from Facebook and Instagram.
 While Facebook said previously that it’s sent  cease and desist letters to Clearview for violating its terms of service, the company has yet to take legal action against the New York City–based startup. Facebook board member Peter Thiel was an early investor in Clearview.
title: Facebook考虑即将面世的智能眼镜的面部识别
title_en: Facebook Considers Facial Recognition for Upcoming Smart Glasses

学生对大学抱有不切实际的期望（可以理解）：不论其全球排名如何，所有大学都将自己视为增长，就业能力和成功的引擎，而大学教育仍然是提升人才的保证。可以理解，这产生了很高的期望，但是要大规模实现它们是不可行的。并非每个人都可以成为领导者，首席执行官，经理或备受追捧的知识工作者。无论从任何客观角度来看，我们在过去100年中都取得了长足的进步，从单调的装配线和日常工作过渡到灵活而有意义的职业和“您的创业”。但请不要忘记，不可能给每个人梦想的工作。如果我们的职业抱负超过了可用的机会，而我们的自我感知的才能超过了我们的实际才能，那么我们肯定注定会在工作中痛苦不堪，这也许可以解释尽管员工投入了越来越多的钱，但员工敬业度却很低员工提供类似消费者的体验。如果每个人都渴望与布拉德·皮特（Brad Pitt）或安吉丽娜·朱莉（Angelina Jolie）等电影明星约会，那么爱情世界中的对等物：结果将是单身人群的流行。
  许多精英大学通常将研究放在优先位置，而这往往以教学为代价：任何在学术界度过时光的人都会知道，至少根据卓越研究表的判断，大学的质量主要基于研究而不是教学。在许多顶级机构中，教学可被视为分散出版和获得研究资助的注意力。顶尖的教师不仅被更高的薪水吸引着，而且还拥有更多的自由和较低的教学负担。作为回报，他们将大量发表研究成果，并获得补助金收入，同时利用研究生进行教学。他们发表研究论文的期刊依赖于一种可疑的商业模式-它们属于获利丰厚的出版帝国所有，它们创造了数十亿美元的收入。
  我们认为，直到整个高等教育系统将教室置于研究实验室之上之前，这种变化的挑战将是一个挑战。主流学术期刊使用的审查程序（独立专家的盲目审查程序）是推进科学的有效途径，但是在知识和开放源代码时代，将基于科学的见解民主化对于那些人来说显然有好处。谁真正资助它。尽管研究是增长与创新的引擎，这说明了顶尖大学对大学的高度重视，但它不应成为忽略为学生提供的实际教育的借口，包括为他们为现实世界做准备的关键问题。同时，将由大学中的各个部门在其重视与激励研究与教学之间取得适当的平衡。
  大学不是在提拔精英能力，而是在加剧不平等现象：大学学位的增值与学生的社会经济地位成反比，因为大多数顶级大学毕业生无论如何都会因其最初的财富，特权，和联系人。这就是使最近的美国大学录取丑闻如此具有讽刺意味的原因：负担得起这么高的贿赂的人是最不需要这样做的人-他们的财富，特权和人际关系实际上保证了他们能够入伍。生活的好地方，无论他们是否上过大学。
  同时，大学倾向于增加而不是减少不平等。正如研究报告所指出的那样，富人不仅更有可能购买更昂贵的教育，而且嫁给了同样富裕和受过教育的人，这反过来又会带来更多的富裕和特权的后代。就像安东尼·杰克（Anthony Jack）在最近的一本书中指出的那样，即使精英大学专注于招收少数族裔，他们也倾向于优先考虑他所谓的“特权穷人”，例如具有较高社会经济地位的黑人或西班牙裔人。我们看到的基本问题是：如果一所大学声称自己是一流的教育机构，它是否不应该录取分数最低的人，并将其转变为明天的领导者（而不是录取分数最高的人）？收入和考试成绩，不管大学三年或四年，谁可能会统治明天？
  简而言之，我们需要重新思考当前的高等教育模式。明天属于与工作同时进行，不断学习的循环中接受教育的公司和个人。未来的成功并不取决于学位，而取决于潜力和学习，运用和适应的能力。
note_en:
On the surface, universities are a nice idea. You go in, pick a subject you like, learn from the experts, and leave being job- and future-ready. This is why so many people (around  40% in rich countries) decide to go to college, even if it means making big financial and personal sacrifices. Yet just because so many people are doing it doesn’t mean it’s necessarily a good thing to do. In fact, while there is generally a cost – in terms of employment prospect – of not having a college degree, there are not always clear competitive advantages in having one, particularly if almost half of the population has one. The reality in today’s digital-first world is that we need to teach every generation how to learn, unlearn, and relearn — quickly — so they can transform the future of work, rather than be transformed by it.
  If you’re reading this, our guess is that you are more likely to have a university degree than not, and we are sure you have very fond memories of your years in college. We also suspect that you have first-hand experience of some of the challenges graduates face when they enter the labor market, and the frustrations employers express when dealing with them (e.g., finding the right people, managing them and their expectations, and developing their skills). For example, many of our ManpowerGroup clients lament the time and money that they must invest in upskilling and reskilling graduates so they can “actually learn skills required to be successful at work versus the skills that made them successful in a classroom.” And most of the students one of us has taught at Columbia and University College London spent a great deal of time trying to find an appealing job despite graduating from top programs, and they always end up having to compromise with their choices.
  No clear alternative to universities has yet emerged, and while there’s no clear path to disrupting higher education, there are pain points which those of us in the education field and beyond could be confronting. At some point a viable alternative will likely emerge and we see six reasons that make the case for demanding something different:
  Employers need skills, not just knowledge or titles: The industrialized world is experiencing an unprecedented  job boom. There has never been a better time in history to find work — this should no doubt be cause for celebration. However, there is still a significant mismatch between the jobs people want and those that are actually available. For example, the current U.S. unemployment rate is just  3.6%, yet there are  7.4 million job openings. Why? First, some of those jobs are unappealing to “overqualified” graduates, which is why Walmart is offering up to  $108K to truck drivers, and still has vacancies. Second, some jobs require a different skillset than what job seekers offer, which is why 60% of organizations can’t find qualified cybersecurity analysts, for example. Third, while the number of college graduates keeps rising, there is a general questioning of how university qualifications translate to work, with a growing number of employers expressing  reservations about graduates’ job-readiness and potential to add immediate value to the workplace. For example, employers often complain that even when graduates come with stellar academic credentials they will probably not have learned what they need to learn to be able to do their job.
  It is also clear that a large number of people often end up in careers that are not even aligned with their education, with a recent report by  Burning Glass suggesting that 20% of graduates are still not working in a degree-demanding job even 10 years post-graduation. Things get even more complicated when we take into consideration the fact that a substantial proportion of future jobs will be hard to predict, except for the fact that they will require a very different range of skills than that displayed by most graduates. That’s why the future potential of the workforce will depend on its ability to cultivate  learnability, rather than displaying lots of college credentials.
  Students want jobs, not knowledge or titles: The number one reason students have for investing so much time and money into a college education is to get a good job, with two thirds of them seeing  “financial stability” as the primary goal. And yet, despite record low unemployment,  under-employment is extremely common, with as many as  40% of college graduates working in jobs that don’t actually require their qualifications. It is also unlikely that students value the actual process of learning — or absorbing knowledge — as much as the actual diploma they receive at the end. For example, would most people rather have an Ivy League education without the diploma, or an Ivy League diploma without the Ivy League education?
  Students are paying more and more to get less and less: Other than healthcare, nothing has risen as much as the cost of higher education, which in the U.S. has  increased some 200% in the past 20 years (145% above the inflation rate). Actually, there is  one thing that has risen far more:  student debt, increased 600%, reaching an all-time-high of $1.4 trillion in America (higher than credit card debt and higher than car financing debt). Believe it or not, some people have managed to accumulate  $1 million in student debt. To be sure, there’s still an ROI to most college degrees, and you are generally better off having one than not having one. However, for every Ivy League degree that  generates around 12% in annual ROI, there are many less prestigious colleges and careers where the balance is negative. It is also true that the more graduates a nation produces, the less value-add there is in being a graduate, which partly explains the forecast that college enrollment should  plateau in the next two or three years.
  Students have unrealistic expectations (understandably) about college: Irrespective of their global ranking, all universities market themselves as an engine of growth, employability, and success, and a college education is still a promise to upgrade someone’s talent. Understandably, this produces high expectations, but it is just not feasible to fulfill them at scale. Not everyone can be a leader, a CEO, a manager, or a highly sought-after knowledge worker. By any objective measure, we’ve come a long way in the past 100 years, transitioning from monotonous assembly lines and routine jobs to flexible and meaningful careers and “the start-up of you.” But let’s not forget that it is just not possible to give everyone their dream job. If our career aspirations surpass the available opportunities, and our self-perceived talents exceed our actual talents, we are surely destined to be miserable at work, and perhaps this explains the prevalence of  low employee engagement ratings despite more and more money being devoted to giving employees a consumer-like experience. The equivalent in the world of love would be if everyone aspired to date movie stars like Brad Pitt or Angelina Jolie: the result would be an epidemic of single people.
  Many elite universities prioritize research, often at the expense of teaching: Anyone who spends time in academia will know that the quality of universities, at least as judged by research excellence tables, is  predominantly based on research rather than teaching. In many top institutions, teaching can be seen as a distraction from publishing and getting research grants. Top faculty are attracted not just with higher salaries, but also with more freedom and a lower teaching load. In return, they will publish research prolifically and bring in grant income while leveraging graduate students to do their teaching instead. And the journals in which they publish that research rely on a questionable business model – they are owned by profitable publishing empires which rake in billions in revenue.
  In our view, until the entire higher educational system prioritizes the classroom over the research lab, it will be a challenge for this dynamic to change. The vetting process used by mainstream academic journals – blind review process by independent experts – is an effective way to advancing science, but in an age of distributed knowledge and open-source information, there is a clear benefit to democratizing science-based insights to those who actually fund it. And while research is the engine of growth and innovation, which explains the strong emphasis top academic universities place on it, it should not be an excuse to neglect the actual education offered to students, including the critical issue of preparing them for the real world. In the meantime, it will be up to individual departments in universities to strike the right balance between their emphasis on and incentives for teaching and research.
  Instead of boosting meritocracy, universities reinforce inequality: The value-add of a university degree is inversely related to a student’s  socio-economic status, as most top college graduates would have enjoyed high levels of career success anyway because of their initial wealth, privilege, and contacts. This is what makes the recent college admissions scandal in the U.S. so  ironic: the people who could afford to pay such high bribes were the ones who needed to do this the least – their wealth, privilege, and contacts virtually guaranteed that they would land in a good place in life regardless of whether or where they attended college.
  At the same time, universities tend to increase rather than decrease inequality. As research  reports have noted, rich people are not just more likely to buy a more expensive education, but also to marry equally rich and educated people, which in turn will produce more affluent and privileged offspring. And as Anthony Jack noted in a recent  book, even when elite universities focus on enrolling minorities, they tend to prioritize what he calls the “privileged poor,” such as Black or Hispanic people from higher socio-economic status. The fundamental question we see is this: If a university claims to be a top educational institution, shouldn’t it admit the people with the lowest test scores, and turn them into the leader of tomorrow (as opposed to admitting the people with the highest income and test scores, who would probably rule the world tomorrow regardless of those three or four years in college)?
  In short, there is much that we need to rethink about the current model of higher education. Tomorrow belongs to the companies and individuals who are approaching education in parallel with work, with continuous loops of learning. Success in the future won’t be defined by a degree, but by potential and the ability to learn, apply, and adapt.
title: 为什么需要中断高等教育
title_en: Why Higher Education Needs to Be Disrupted

在卖掉用大麻籽油制成的巧克力棒和布朗尼食品后，一个现年62岁的马来西亚祖父，亲切地称为Ganja博士，在一个大麻（甚至出于医疗目的）仍然是非法且高度禁忌的国家面临死刑判决。
   阿米鲁丁·纳达拉让·阿卜杜拉（Amiruddin Nadarajan Abdullah）是五岁的祖父，曾在马来西亚军队服役22年，是此案的中心，此举使他成为马来西亚最著名的医用大麻合法化支持者之一。
  “他不是毒贩，也不是某些毒drug。”他的女儿西蒂（Siti）曾在当地的银行业务部门工作，他对《 VICE世界新闻》说。
  “他是一个富有创造力的人，喜欢诗歌和阅读，但他也受科学的驱使，在思考和对待事物方面非常现代。”
  继去年联合国决定承认大麻的药用特性之后，逐步发展的毒品政策倡导者希望，随着数十亿美元的全球医用大麻产业的持续发展，世界各国将对这种药物的态度不断追赶。
  然而，马来西亚拥有世界上最严格的禁毒法，与该地区放宽禁令甚至邻国泰国更近的禁令形成鲜明对比。关于大麻，医疗或娱乐性的公开讨论在马来西亚及其贩运仍然是禁忌，应处以死刑。
   但是，激进主义者和地方倡导团体仍然充满希望，并表示在这个以穆斯林为主的保守国家中，公众的态度正在发生变化，前改革派政府的政客甚至提议立法将医用大麻合法化。 
其中一位是马来西亚前青年部长赛义德·萨迪克（Syed Saddiq），他在2018年被任命后寻求机会，努力推动这一问题的发展。
  他在一封电子邮件声明中告诉《 VICE世界新闻》：“医用大麻的非刑事化有很多优点，即使在马来西亚，这个想法也不是新鲜的-如果我们有更多的时间，我们肯定会采取必要的措施。”
  “出于医疗消费的目的，将大麻罪犯合法化仍然需要做。现任政府应向有需要的人提供使用医用大麻的权利，而不是对其进行起诉和惩罚。”
  但是Ganja博士介入这场辩论是偶然的。根据西蒂的说法，在1990年代后期，他因一系列健康问题而困扰，例如肾脏问题，糖尿病和心脏病，他在短短几个月内就从一名硬汉军人变成了“活尸”。
  最终他被诊断出患有痛苦的脊髓肿瘤，致使他失去了肾脏。
  像其他患有慢性疼痛和癌症相关疲劳的人一样，甘贾博士也在寻找替代疗法，不久就被带到了医用大麻的兔子洞里，当他遇到大麻籽油时，在网上论坛上浏览了数小时，并决定尝试一下。 。
  Siti说：“我们看到了产品带来的好处和缓解，” Siti指的是来自大麻植物不同部位的油，一些研究表明，这种油可以减轻化疗带来的副作用。 
Siti表示，医生开始看到显着的改善，他说X射线显示他的肿瘤缩小了，并且疼痛减轻了很多。
  “发现给他带来了新的生机。这令人鼓舞，”她说。
  转换为大麻的医疗福利后，甘贾博士开始欢迎癌症患者以及患有其他身体和精神疾病的患者来到他在巴生区的乡村住宅。
   他相信大麻的治疗特性，并经常将其用于布朗尼，巧克力和药用蜂蜜等食品中。除家庭面包店外，他还经营了一个Facebook页面，在其中分享“成功案例”，并倡导医用大麻的合法化和非刑事化。
  2017年，根据马来西亚《危险药物法》，Ganja博士被捕，并被指控犯有36项罪行。
  联邦麻醉药品和刑事调查局局长在首都吉隆坡举行的审前会议上说：“该人通过宣传大麻和大麻籽的好处在社交媒体上赢得了广泛的公众关注。”
  “他声称他的产品可以治愈疾病，他的意图是帮助人们。” 
他现在正在双溪毛糯监狱综合大楼内等待判决，这是马来西亚最大的监狱设施之一，成群的犯人对COVID-19进行了阳性测试。
  最新的照片显示，一个年迈体弱的老人被铐在轮椅上，并在12月的警察陪同下出席法庭听证会，这可能是他几个月来的最后一次出庭约会，因为大流行减慢了司法系统的速度。
    他的律师拉姆卡帕尔·辛格（Ramkarpal Singh）也是反对派政治家，在接受《 VICE世界新闻》采访时说，由于他面临长期指控，该案仍在审理中，但他尚未被定罪。
  辛格说：“此案的独特性在于他先前的医疗状况，但据他所知，他认为自己没有做任何违法的事情。”
  “他面临死刑，但我们希望使他获得无罪释放，并减少他的指控，但由于要检查的证物和证人数量众多，预计这将需要相当长的时间。”
  人权组织估计，由于毒品犯罪，马来西亚仍有1000多人被判死刑。目前已暂停执行死刑，许多人的命运也因与Ganja博士类似的罪行而被定罪，但仍悬而未决。
  其中之一是马来西亚大麻教育运动的创始人穆罕默德·扎伊尔·本·扎纳尔（Mohammed Zaireen bin Zainal），他正在等待最终上诉。来自槟城岛的一位椰奶销售商最近也在9月因在当地美食广场出售大麻而被判处死刑。 
但是这个问题也看到了最近的积极事态发展，例如一名30岁男子在2015年成功上诉死刑后设法逃脱绞刑架的案件，这在马来西亚倡导团体和国际观察员中重新燃起了希望。
  “大马政府必须继续努力，废除对包括毒品贩运在内的所有犯罪的强制性死刑，并审查导致全国监狱人满为患的政策和法律，”大赦马来西亚执行董事卡特里娜·马利亚毛努（Katrina Maliamauv）说。
  来自马来西亚意识协会的Harish Kumar开展运动，争取该国医用大麻的合法化，他强调需要进行更多的改变。
  “大麻是药用植物，就这么简单。我们需要摆脱对毒品的公众污名化，这种污名在许多马来西亚人中仍然很普遍。”
   在泰国于2018年成为第一个东南亚国家后，马来西亚就曾有过关于将大麻完全合法化以实现药用目的的讨论，但库玛说，在政治动荡之后，大麻运动停滞不前，并强调了政府仍然需要制定关键性支持议会中的“有意义的改变”。
     为争取Ganja博士的自由而奋斗的是三位年轻的当地电影摄制人Jo Yee Loh，Hidayah Hisham和Dominique Teoh，他们最近发行了一部关于家人困境的完整纪录片。
  该组织告诉《 VICE世界新闻》：“他的故事不仅是一场医学辩论，而且也影响了马来西亚有关人权的讨论和辩论。” 
“整个马来西亚的成千上万的家庭，像他的一样，都受到毒品法的影响。必须让更多的马来西亚人相信大麻不是危险药物，应该允许需要大麻的人合法使用大麻，而不是受到迫害和惩罚。”
  由于仍然有严格的监狱探访限制，Siti已经有几个月没有收到父亲的消息了。
  她说，他的每周打来的电话已经停止，她仍然担心他在监狱中的健康。 12月，泌尿道感染，频繁的腹痛和流感恐慌使该家庭担心他的健康和安全，因为据报多个监狱都爆发了冠状病毒。
  西蒂说：“我为父亲而战，并为他离开的每一天哀悼。”
  “但是除非我们的法律有所改变，否则任何事情都不会改变，我无法撼动他已经不在我们身边的感觉。”
  签署VICE新闻通讯，即表示您同意接收来自VICE的电子通讯，其中有时可能包含广告或赞助内容。
note_en:
After selling chocolate bars and brownie edibles made with hemp seed oil, a 62-year-old Malaysian grandfather, known affectionately as Dr Ganja, faces the death sentence in a country where marijuana—even for  medicinal purposes—remains illegal and highly taboo.
   Amiruddin Nadarajan Abdullah, a grandfather of five who served in the Malaysian army for 22 years, is at the center of a case that catapulted him to national prominence as one of Malaysia’s most famous proponents of medical marijuana legalization.
  “He isn’t a drug dealer or some drug lord,” his daughter Siti, who works as a local banking officer, told VICE World News.
  “He’s a creative man who loves poetry and reading, but he’s also driven by science and is very modern in his thinking and approach to things.”
  Following a United Nations decision last year to  recognize the medicinal properties of cannabis, progressive drug policy advocates hoped countries around the world would catch up with evolving attitudes towards the substance, as a multi-billion dollar global medical marijuana industry continues to expand.
  Malaysia, however, has some of the strictest drug laws in the world and stands in stark contrast to eased restrictions around the region and even closer to home in neighboring  Thailand. Open discussions about marijuana, medical or recreational, remain taboo in Malaysia and the trafficking of it, punishable by death.
   But activists and local advocacy groups remain hopeful, and say that public attitudes in the conservative and predominantly Muslim country are changing, with politicians from the previous reformist government even  proposing legislation to decriminalize medical marijuana.
  One of them was  Syed Saddiq, Malaysia’s former youth minister, who sought the opportunity upon his appointment in 2018 to try and push the issue forward.
  “There are plenty of pros for the decriminalization of medical marijuana and the idea was not new, even in Malaysia—and we would definitely have taken the steps needed if we were given more time,” he told VICE World News in an emailed statement.
  “Decriminalizing marijuana offenders for the purpose of medical consumption still needs to be done. The current government should accord access to medical marijuana to those who need it, not prosecute them and punish them for it.”
  But Dr Ganja’s involvement in this debate came about by chance. Plagued by a series of health issues like kidney problems, diabetes and heart disease in the late 1990s, he went from being a hale army man to “a living corpse” in a span of mere months, according to Siti.
  He was eventually diagnosed with a painful spinal cord tumor that caused him to lose a kidney.
  Like others who struggle with chronic pain and cancer-related fatigue, Dr Ganja went in search of alternative treatments and was soon led down the rabbit hole of  medical cannabis, surfing online forums for hours when he came across hemp seed oil and decided to try it.
  “We saw the benefits and relief that the products brought him,” Siti said, referring to the oil from different parts of the cannabis plant, which some studies have shown to offer  relief from the side effects of chemotherapy.
  Doctors began seeing remarkable improvements, according to Siti, who said X-rays showed that his tumours shrank and he was experiencing much less pain.
  “The discovery gave him a new lease of life. It was inspiring,” she said.
  A convert to the medical benefits of marijuana, Dr Ganja started welcoming cancer patients as well as those suffering from other physical and mental health conditions to his village home in the district of Klang.
   He believed in the healing properties of cannabis and often used it in edibles like brownies, chocolates and medicinal honey. In addition to his home bakery, he also ran a Facebook page where he shared “success stories” and advocated for the legalization and decriminalization of medical marijuana.
  In 2017, Dr Ganja was arrested and charged with 36 offences under Malaysia’s Dangerous Drugs Act.
  “The man garnered a large public following on social media by promoting the benefits of ganja and hemp seeds,” directors from the Federal Narcotics and Criminal Investigation Department told a  pretrial conference held in the capital Kuala Lumpur.
  “He claims that his products can cure illness and his intention was to help people.”
  He is now awaiting sentencing in Sungai Buloh prison complex, one of Malaysia’s largest prison facilities where groups of inmates have  tested positive for COVID-19.
  Latest photos showed a frail and elderly man, handcuffed to a wheelchair and accompanied by police officers to a court hearing in December, which would turn out to be his last court date for months as the pandemic slowed down the judicial system.
    In an interview with VICE World News, his lawyer Ramkarpal Singh, also an opposition politician, said that the case was still ongoing due to the lengthy charges he was facing, but that he had not been convicted yet.
  “The uniqueness of the case lies in his pre-existing medical conditions, but from his knowledge, he didn’t think he was doing anything illegal,” Singh said.
  “He is facing the death penalty but we hope to get him acquitted and his charges reduced but that is expected to take quite some time because of the amount of exhibits and witnesses to examine.”
  Rights groups estimate that  more than 1,000 people remain on death row in Malaysia over drug offences. A moratorium on executions is currently in place, with the fate of many convicted on similar offences as Dr Ganja, still hanging in the balance.
  Among those is Mohammed Zaireen bin Zainal, founder of the Malaysian Marijuana Education Movement, who is awaiting a final appeal. A coconut milk seller from the island of Penang was also recently sentenced to death in September for  selling cannabis at a local food court.
   But the issue has also seen recent positive developments, like the case of a 30-year-old man who managed to  escape the gallows after successfully appealing his death sentence in 2015, reigniting hope among Malaysian advocacy groups as well as international observers.
  “The Malaysian government must continue efforts to repeal the mandatory death penalty for all crimes including drug trafficking, and review the policies and laws that have led to the  overcrowding of prisons across the country,” said Katrina Maliamauv, executive director for Amnesty Malaysia.
  Harish Kumar, from the  Malaysia Society of Awareness which campaigns for the legalization of medical marijuana in the country, stressed that more change was needed.
  “Cannabis is a medicinal plant, it’s as simple as that. We need to get rid of the public stigma around drugs, which is still prevalent among many Malaysians,” Kumar said.
   There had been previous talk of fully legalizing cannabis for medicinal purposes in Malaysia after Thailand became the first Southeast Asian country in 2018 to do so — but Kumar said that movements stalled after  political upheaval and highlighted crucial support that was still needed from the government to enact “meaningful change” in parliament.
     Campaigning for Dr Ganja’s freedom are three young local filmmakers, Jo Yee Loh, Hidayah Hisham and Dominique Teoh, who recently released a  full-length documentary about his family’s plight.
  “His story isn’t just a medical debate, it’s one that affects the discussion and debate about human rights in Malaysia too,” the group told VICE World News.
  “Thousands of families across Malaysia, like his, have been affected by the drug laws. More Malaysians need to be convinced that marijuana isn’t a dangerous drug and people who need it should be allowed legitimate access, not be persecuted and punished.”
  With heavy restrictions around prison visits still in place, Siti hasn’t heard from her father in months.
  She said that weekly phone calls from him had stopped, and she remained worried about his health in prison. Urinary tract infections, frequent abdominal pains and a flu scare in December left the family fearing for his health and safety as coronavirus outbreaks were reported across multiple prisons.
  “I’m fighting for my father and mourn every day that he’s away,” Siti said.
  “But nothing will change unless our laws change and I can’t shake the feeling that he’s already beyond our reach.”
  By signing up to the VICE newsletter you agree to receive electronic communications from VICE that may sometimes include advertisements or sponsored content.
title: 马来西亚祖父因杂草面临死亡
title_en: Malaysian Grandfather Is Facing Death for Weed

note:
零排放卡车公司Nikola已关闭其所谓的Powersports部门，并暂停了2019年4月首次宣布的电动个人船只和越野车的工作。在2020年发生了许多失误之后，它的第一台氢动力卡车就出局了。根据监管文件，该决定使这家初创公司损失了1,440万美元。
 “我们仍然拥有NZT和WAV的权利，并已暂停该项目。一位发言人告诉The Verge，我们可能会考虑在以后与他们一起前进。 “目前，我们专注于商用卡车和氢能基础设施。”
   尼古拉（Nikola）收购了从事这一想法的最有希望的初创公司之一后，于2017年开始购买电动水上摩托车。越野车是各种混合动力，将沙丘越野车的造型与乘用车（如空调）中的舒适感混合在一起。尼古拉曾承诺，所谓的NZT车辆将获得590马力和150英里的射程。它本应于今年上市，起价为80,000美元。
 这两款Powersports车辆都是Nikola在2019年年度盛会上的重点，但是考虑到国防工业的兴起，尤其是NZT似乎总是对于初创公司来说是一个赚钱的机会。
 Powersports部门并不是Nikola艰难的2020年的第一伤亡。这家初创公司的皮卡车项目在与通用汽车（General Motors）陷入困境之后崩溃了，后者在指控欺诈行为开始之前就支持了Nikola。
 尼古拉周四下午宣布其Powersports部门死亡，并宣布其2020年财务业绩。该公司报告今年亏损3.84亿美元，尽管它在2020年底完成了8.4亿美元的银行存款，这要归功于它在今年早些时候上市时产生的钱。
 美国东部时间2月25日晚上10:30更新：添加了关闭Powersports部门费用的信息。
note_en:
Zero-emission trucking company Nikola has shuttered its so-called Powersports division and is pausing work on an electric personal watercraft and off-road vehicle  first announced in April 2019. Nikola stopped work on the projects as part of  a larger push to focus on getting its first hydrogen-powered truck out the door, following a number of stumbles in 2020. The decision cost the startup $14.4 million,  according to a regulatory filing.
 “We still own the NZT and WAV rights and have put the projects on pause. We may consider moving forward with them at a later time,” a spokesperson tells  The Verge. “Right now we are focusing on commercial trucking and hydrogen infrastructure.”
   Nikola started pursuing an electric watercraft back in 2017 after it bought up  one of the more promising startups working on the idea. The off-road vehicle was a hybrid of sorts, mixing dune buggy styling with the kinds of comforts found in passenger cars, like air conditioning. Nikola was promising the so-called NZT vehicle would get 590 horsepower and 150 miles of range. It was supposed to come to market this year at a starting price of $80,000.
 Both Powersports vehicles were a big focus of Nikola’s annual event in 2019, but the NZT in particular always seemed like a possible moneymaker for the startup considering the  interest it generated from the defense industry.
 The Powersports division is not the first casualty of Nikola’s rocky 2020. The startup’s  pickup truck project crumbled after a falling out with General Motors, which was backing Nikola before accusations of fraud started flying.
 Nikola announced the death of its Powersports division Thursday afternoon along with its  2020 financial results. The company reported a $384 million loss for the year, though it finished 2020 with $840 million in the bank,  thanks to the money it generated when it went public earlier in the year.
 Update February 25th, 10:30PM ET:  Added information about the cost of shuttering the Powersports division.
title: 尼古拉停止电动船只和全地形车项目的工作
title_en: Nikola Stops Work on Electric Watercraft and ATV Projects

十多年来，GamersNexus一直是我们RSS提要的主要内容。该网站已迅速成为任何想要构建PC（尤其是游戏PC）的人的必读材料。除了经营这家企业外，首席掌舵人史蒂夫·伯克（Steve Burke）的编辑最近也成为我们每周观看的主要内容，掌舵GamersNexus＆＃39;同样受欢迎的YouTube频道。
  无需花费很多时间就可以认识到GamersNexus与Orbital HQ共享许多DNA。在每一个视频中，伯克和他的团队都进行了交流和娱乐，既没有技术术语，也没有创造有用的redmeit meme的机会。到目前为止，GamersNexus的视频集中于从沃尔玛（Walmart）的PC到真正的技术步伐，到在不良的CPU上倒空（实际上就是倒空）一堆导热膏的一切内容。每次您都会学到有用的信息，即使这不是令人垂涎的新部件。 ＆＃34;我认为我们在视频中说得很清楚，但是如果有人试图构建PC并想知道，嗯，我该如何涂抹导热膏？他们输入＆＃39;导热膏，＆＃39;他们点击了该视频，然后观看了五秒钟，然后走了，噢，我明白了。我不需要本教程的其余部分，＆＃34;＆＃39;伯克在我们最近的《个人历史》采访中告诉Ars。我认为，否则它们将拥有一个非常凌乱的CPU插槽。 （顺便说一句，伯克（Burke）提出了实际应用导热硅脂的最佳方法是快速制作X。＆＃34;它使我可以将CPU的频率提高到5GhZ，6GhZ，没问题。）在以上25分钟的聊天中，Burke慷慨地使用了液氮，分享了制作成功的操作视频的技巧，并就所有黑色配线到处理电源中的错误等问题进行了故障排除。来讨论CPU风扇，继续学习为什么组件术语是一种真正的通用语言。
note_en:
GamersNexus has been a staple of our RSS feeds for more than a decade. The site has quickly become a must read for anyone looking to build a PC, especially a  gaming PC. And in addition to running that enterprise, Editor in Chief Steve Burke has more recently become a staple of our weekly viewing, too, as he helms  GamersNexus' equally popular YouTube channel.
  It doesn't take a lot of time to realize GamersNexus clearly shares a lot of DNA with the Orbital HQ. In every video, Burke and his team both inform and entertain, skimping neither technical jargon nor opportunities  to create useful reddit memes. By now, GamersNexus videos have focused on everything from  putting PCs from Walmart through genuine technical paces to emptying ( literally,  emptying) a tube of thermal paste on a poor CPU. You'll learn useful info every time, even if it's  what new parts  not to covet.     "I think we made it pretty clear in the video, but if someone is trying to build a PC and wonders, 'Hmm, how do I apply thermal paste?" they type in 'thermal paste,' they click on this video, and then they watch five seconds and go, 'Oh, I get it. I don't need the rest of this tutorial,"' Burke told Ars in our recent Personal History interview. "Otherwise they'd have a pretty messy CPU socket, I think." (Burke, by the way, suggests the best way to actually apply thermal paste is just by making a quick X. "It allows me to get CPUs up to 5GhZ, 6GhZ, no problem.") In our 25 minute chat above, Burke is generous with the liquid nitrogen, shares tips for making successful how-to videos, and fields troubleshooting questions on everything from all black wiring to handling dead bugs in the power supply. Come for the discussion of CPU fans, stay for learning why component jargon is the one true universal language.
title: GamersNexus的Steve Burke超频了他的YouTube频道的最佳评论
title_en: GamersNexus’ Steve Burke overclocks his YouTube channel’s best comments

风险投资也是如此，尽管对于Brookfield来说，风险还很陌生，所以可能希望获得风险投资的创始人仍能得到这份备忘录。的确，不到四年前，布鲁克菲尔德技术合作伙伴（Brookfield Technology Partners）开始对公司的资产负债表进行投资，并在招聘了斯坦福MBA乔什·拉法里（Josh Raffaelli）之后不久，他在德雷珀·费舍尔·于韦森（Draper Fisher Jurvetson）担任校长，随后又花了五年时间银湖-领导实践。
 实际上，它的存在令他感到惊讶。 “我一直是硅谷的技术投资者，” Raffaelli说。 “从我在[Bay Area]住的房子开车到我的整个职业生涯都只有15分钟的车程。在布鲁克菲尔德开始从事这项业务之前，我从未听说过布鲁克菲尔德，因为这是在企业中开展的。在房地产中。它所做的事情通常不是技术支持的。”
  直到最近，才是。自那时以来，Raffaelli和他的11人团队不仅下了数十笔赌注，而且他们目前正在从拥有布鲁克菲尔德之外的第三方资本的资本池中进行投资（这是首创）。至于他们要寻找的内容，其目的是帮助Brookfield重新设想其许多办公楼，购物中心和其他房地产可以被使用，开发，租赁或保险的方式。这是为了使Brookfield变得更聪明，准备更充分，利润更高。作为回报，这些初创公司获得了行业专业知识-并且是Brookfield的主要客户。
   迄今为止，它的赌注变化很大，例如专注于非托管设备安全性的物联网初创公司Armis。 Loanpal，太阳能和其他家用节能产品的销售点付款平台；和Carbon Health，这是一家结合了真实世界和虚拟访问的初级保健公司。 ““我们正在从Brookfield生态系统中有效地获取主题，” Raffaelli说。
 拉斐尔（Raffaelli）说，他的团队将支票从2500万美元增加到5000万美元，他们希望收入在1000万美元以上的公司的年同比增长率超过100％。在节奏方面，他们每季度大约达成一项新交易。
 该基金也是独立的，拥有自己的自定义委员会，但该委员会由布鲁克菲尔德各行各业的高级管理合伙人组成。 （“这些人实际上是在帮助我们翻译我们在这里产生的投资主题的人，” Raffaelli指出。）
 为了突出该操作的工作方式，Raffaelli指出了智能门禁软件业务Latch，该公司上个月宣布它正在使用一家由房地产巨头Tishman Speyer支持的空白支票公司进行公开交易。布鲁克菲尔德在北美拥有大约70,000个多户家庭单位，“所以我们有很多门需要很多锁，”拉斐利说。当然，闩锁并不是那里唯一的智能访问锁，因此，布鲁克菲尔德（Brookfield）进行了“几乎就像一个微型[提案流程]的活动，向市场上所有不同的公司伸出援手，以了解他们如何竞争，”他说。 
这是一个“六个月的练习”，但最终，他的团队领导了Latch的2018年B轮融资，此后，布鲁克菲尔德被该公司收购了约7,000个街区。 Raffaelli说，考虑到当Brookfield最初投资时，该公司的预订额不到2000万美元，而那7000个锁具又带来了1000万至1500万美元的额外收入，这是一个有意义的区别。他补充说：“当我们在公司的那个阶段购买很多东西时，我们正在有意义地改善他们的发展轨迹。”
  这不是万无一失的策略，请加倍考虑。如果Latch的锁真是柠檬（不是柠檬），那么Brookfield的资本支出将是一大笔钱。 Raffaelli说，这就是Brookfield花时间的原因，并补充说，如果他做得很好，他的团队将在参与一轮融资之前很早就加入了一家公司，并已经表明它是“具有另一种杠杆作用的战略合作伙伴”。
 无论哪种方式，Raffaelli都说，尽管流行病对商业房地产市场造成了沉重打击，但与他的团队相反，这对他的团队来说是一个富有成效的时期，因为它给予了房地产行业更快地采用技术工具的更大动力。 Raffaelli眼下最激动的赌注是VTS，例如可以远程显示资产的租赁和资产管理平台，以及Raffaelli形容为“适用于其他所有人的Amazon Prime”的电子商务履行初创公司Deliverr。
 实际上，Raffaelli令人信服地指出，尽管许多房地产的用例正在发生变化，但鉴于其占地面积，所谓的建筑世界仍然是布鲁克菲尔德最强大的竞争优势。他的看法及其未来的选择很多。 “您正在寻找成为幽灵厨房的零售场所；您正在寻找的零售地点将变成分销和物流设施。我们可以将实体位置变成[我们的投资组合公司] Carbon Health的医疗保健场所，并将我们的购物中心位置变成紧急护理和基层医疗诊所进行测试和接种疫苗的场所。”
 这将永远不是一个完全无缝的过渡。考虑到大流行及其破坏性影响，布鲁克菲尔德也必须“考虑周全”。但是Raffaelli在对话中还是很兴奋。 Raffaelli补充说，将有形房地产变成“技术行业内变革的机制”的想法是“非常强大的地方”。
note_en:
That’s also true of venture capital, though venture is new enough to Brookfield that founders who might like its capital are still getting the memo. Indeed, it was a little less than four years ago that Brookfield Technology Partners began investing off the company’s balance sheet and soon after recruited Josh Raffaelli — a Stanford MBA who cut his teeth as a principal with Draper Fisher Jurvetson, then spent another five years with Silver Lake — to lead the practice.
 Its existence came as a surprise to him, actually. “I’ve been a tech investor in Silicon Valley,” says Raffaelli. “My entire professional career has been in a 15-minute drive from the [Bay Area] house I grew up in. And I had never heard about Brookfield before they started this practice because it’s in businesses. It’s in real estate. It has done things that are not generally tech-enabled.”
  Not until fairly recently, that is. Raffaelli and his 11-person team have not only made dozens of bets since then, but they’re currently investing out of a pool of capital that features third party capital in addition to that of Brookfield — which is a first. As for what they are looking for, the idea is help Brookfield reimagine how its many office towers, malls and other real estate might be used or developed or leased or insured. It’s to make Brookfield smarter, better prepared, and more profitable. In return, the startups get industry expertise — and a major customer in Brookfield.
   To date, its bets have varied widely, as with  Armis, an IoT startup focused on unmanaged device security;  Loanpal, a point-of-sale payment platform for solar and other home efficiency products; and  Carbon Health, a primary care company that blends real-world and virtual visits. “”We’re getting our themes effectively from the Brookfield ecosystem,” Raffaelli says.
 Pulling back the curtain a bit more, Raffaelli says his team writes checks from $25 million to $50 million dollars and that they look for companies with $10 million in revenue that are seeing top-line year-over-year growth of more than 100%. In terms of pacing, they jump into roughly one new deal per quarter.
 The fund is also independent and has its own custom committee, but that the committee is made up of the senior managing partners from each line of Brookfield’s businesses. (“These are the people that actually help us translate our investment themes that we’re generating here,” Raffaelli notes.)
 To highlight how the operation works, Raffaelli points to  Latch, a smart access software business that announced  last month that it’s using a blank-check company backed by the real estate giant Tishman Speyer to become publicly traded. Brookfield owns roughly 70,000 multifamily units in North America, “so we have a lot of doors that need a lot of locks,” Raffaelli says. Latch, of course, is not the only smart access lock out there, so Brookfield ran “what was almost like a mini [proposal process], reaching out to all different companies in the market to understand how they compete,” he says.
 It was a “six-month exercise,” but ultimately, his group led Latch’s Series B round in 2018 and since then, Brookfield was bought about 7,000 blocks from the business. It’s a meaningful difference, considering that when Brookfield first invested, the company had less than $20 million in bookings and those 7,000 locks have since brought in an additional $10 million to $15 million in revenue, Raffaelli says. “When we buy a lot of things at that stage of a company,” he adds, “we’re meaningfully enhancing their trajectory.”
  It’s not a foolproof strategy, doubling down. If Latch’s locks turned out to be lemons (they haven’t), Brookfield would be out a big check along with that capital expenditure. It’s why Brookfield takes its time, says Raffaelli, adding that if he has done his job right, his team is involved with a company well before it is raising a round and shown already that it is a “strategic partner that has another lever.”
 Either way, Raffaelli says that while the commercial real estate market has been hard hit by the pandemic, it has, counterintuitively, been a productive time for his group given the stronger incentive it has given the real estate world to adopt tech tools faster. Among the bets about which Raffaelli sounds most excited right now is  VTS, for example, a leasing and asset management platform that can show properties remotely, and  Deliverr, an e-commerce fulfillment startup that Raffaelli describes as “Amazon Prime for everybody else.”
 In fact, Raffaelli convincingly argues that while the use case for a lot of real estate is changing,  the so-called built world remains Brookfield’s strongest competitive advantage given the size of its footprint.  The way he sees it, its options going forward are plentiful. “You’re looking at retail locations becoming ghost kitchens; you’re looking at retail locations turning into distribution and logistics facilities. We can turn physical locations into healthcare sites for [our portfolio company] Carbon Health, and our mall locations into locations for urgent care and primary care clinics for testing and vaccinations.”
 It will never be a completely seamless transition. Brookfield has to be “thoughtful” given the pandemic and its devastating impacts, too. But Raffaelli comes across as excited in conversation nonetheless. The idea of turning physical real estate into a “mechanism for change within technology businesses,” adds Raffaelli, is a “very powerful place to be.”
title: 如何从布鲁克菲尔德资产管理公司获得启动资金，该公司管理着6,000亿美元的资产

许多月前，Declan Eardly询问为什么选择\字符作为路径分隔符。这一切都源于Microsoft与IBM的关系。对于DOS 1.0，DOS仅支持软盘。
 许多DOS实用程序（command.com除外）都是由IBM编写的，它们使用了＆＃34; /＆＃34;。字符作为＆＃34;开关＆＃34;实用程序的字符（“开关”字符是用于区分命令行开关的字符-在* nix上，它是“-”的字符，在大多数DEC操作系统（包括VMS，DECSystem-20和DECSystem-10）上，它是＆＃34; /＆＃34;字符（注意：是否为灰色＆＃34; /＆＃34;字符来自IBM或Microsoft-一些最初的MS-DOS开发人员是旧的DEC-20开发人员，因此他们有可能将其从DEC继承下来背景）。
 ＆＃34; /＆＃34;与另一个相对流行的操作系统的路径字符冲突的字符与原始开发人员并不特别相关-毕竟，DOS不支持目录，仅支持单个根目录中的文件。
 然后出现了DOS 2.0。 DOS 2.0与PC / XT绑定在一起，后者的主要功能是10M硬盘。 IBM要求Microsoft添加对硬盘的支持，而MS-DOS开发人员以此为契机添加了对现代文件API的支持-他们向系统添加了一系列基于句柄的API（DOS 1.0依赖于受控制的应用程序结构，称为FCB）。他们还必须增加对分层路径的支持。
 从历史上看，现在已经有许多不同的机制可用于提供分层路径。例如，DecSystem-20将目录表示为：＆＃34;＆lt; volume＆gt;：＆＃34;＆lt;＆＃34;＆lt; Directory＆gt; [。＆lt; Subdirectory＆gt;＆＃34;＆gt;＆＃34; FileName.Extension [，Version]＆＃34; （＆＃34; PS：＆lt; SYSTEM＆gt; MONITR.EXE，4＆＃34;）。 VMS使用了类似的命名方案，但不是＆lt;和＆gt;它使用[和]字符（VMS使用＆＃34 ;;＆＃34;来区分文件的版本）。 * nix使用以＆＃34; /＆＃34;为根的简单层次结构定义层次结构路径。 -在* nix的命名层次结构中，没有办法区分文件和目录等（这很不错，顺便说一句，就是这样）。
 对于MS-DOS 2.0，DOS的设计者选择了一个混合版本-他们已经支持DOS 1.0中的驱动器号，因此他们需要继续使用它。他们选择使用* nix样式方法指定层次结构-而不是在文件名中调用目录（例如VMS和DEC-20），他们只是使目录和文件名无法区分路径。
 但有一个问题。他们不能使用＆＃34; /＆＃34;的路径分隔符的* nix形式，因为＆＃34; /＆＃34;被用作切换字符。 
那他们该怎么办？他们本可以使用＆＃34;。＆＃34;字符，例如DEC机器，但＆＃34;。＆＃34;字符用于区分文件和扩展名。因此，他们选择了下一个最好的东西-＆＃34; \＆＃34;字符，在视觉上类似于＆＃34; /＆＃34;特点。
  这是关于MS-DOS的一个鲜为人知的秘密。 DOS开发人员对这种情况并不特别满意-哎呀，他们都使用Xenix机器来发送电子邮件和其他东西，因此他们熟悉* nix命令的语义。因此，他们对操作系统进行了编码，以接受＆＃34; /＆＃34;或＆＃34; \＆＃34;字符作为路径字符（顺便说一句，今天继续进行-尝试在XP计算机上键入“记事本c：/boot.ini”（如果您是管理员））。他们又走了一步。他们添加了一个未公开的系统调用来更改开关字符。并更新了实用程序以遵守此标志。

 从而将MS-DOS转换为* nix样式的系统，其中命令行使用＆＃34; -switch＆＃34 ;，并且对路径进行了/分隔。

note_en:
Many, many months ago, Declan Eardly asked why the \ character was chosen as the path separator.   It all stems from Microsoft's relationship with IBM.  For DOS 1.0, DOS only supported floppy disks.
 Many of the DOS utilities (except for command.com) were written by IBM, and they used the "/" character as the "switch" character for their utilities (the "switch" character is the character that's used to distinguish command line switches - on *nix, it's the "-" character, on most DEC operating systems (including VMS, the DECSystem-20 and DECSystem-10), it's the "/" character" (note: I'm grey on whether the "/" character came from IBM or from Microsoft - several of the original MS-DOS developers were old-hand DEC-20 developers, so it's possible that they carried it forward from their DEC background).
 The fact that the "/" character conflicted with the path character of another relatively popular operating system wasn't particularly relevant to the original developers - after all, DOS didn't support directories, just files in a single root directory.
 Then along came DOS 2.0.  DOS 2.0 was tied to the PC/XT, whose major feature was a 10M hard disk.  IBM asked the Microsoft to add support for hard disks, and the MS-DOS developers took this as an opportunity to add support for modern file APIs - they added a whole series of handle based APIs to the system (DOS 1.0 relied on an application controlled structure called an FCB).  They also had to add support for hierarchical paths.
 Now historically there have been a number of different mechanisms for providing hierarchical paths.  The DecSystem-20, for example represented directories as: "<volume>:"<"<Directory>[.<Subdirectory>">"FileName.Extension[,Version]" ("PS:<SYSTEM>MONITR.EXE,4").   VMS used a similar naming scheme, but instead of < and > characters it used [ and ] (and VMS used ";" to differentiate between versions of files).  *nix defines hierarchical paths with a simple hierarchy rooted at "/" - in *nix's naming hierarchy, there's no way of differentiating between files and directories, etc (this isn't bad, btw, it just is).
 For MS-DOS 2.0, the designers of DOS chose a hybrid version - they already had support for drive letters from DOS 1.0, so they needed to continue using that.  And they chose to use the *nix style method of specifying a hierarchy - instead of calling the directory out in the filename (like VMS and the DEC-20), they simply made the directory and filename indistinguishable parts of the path.
 But there was a problem.  They couldn't use the *nix form of path separator of "/", because the "/" was being used for the switch character.
 So what were they to do?  They could have used the "." character like the DEC machines, but the "." character was being used to differentiate between file and extension.  So they chose the next best thing - the "\" character, which was visually similar to the "/" character.
  Here's a little known secret about MS-DOS.  The DOS developers weren't particularly happy about this state of affairs - heck, they all used Xenix machines for email and stuff, so they were familiar with the *nix command semantics.  So they coded the OS to accept either "/" or "\" character as the path character (this continues today, btw - try typing "notepad c:/boot.ini"  on an XP machine (if you're an admin)).  And they went one step further.  They added an undocumented system call to change the switch character.  And updated the utilities to respect this flag.
 And then they went and finished out the scenario:  They added a config.sys option, SWITCHAR= that would let you set the switch character to "-".
 Which flipped MS-DOS into a *nix style system where command lines used "-switch", and paths were / delimited.
 I don't know the fate of the switchar API, it's been long gone for many years now.
   For a similar article see entry III.15 in this  http://netwhatever.com/faq/inicio.html  Also to note, / was a common command-line-option indicator in CP/M, and QDOS (which Dos1 was of course based off of) was very much a clone of CP/M.
  Interesting, I had always wondered why DOS used something different wen UNIX was well established and accepted standard and I sometimes wondered if it was different just for the sake of being different.  Glad to see that assumption was wrong.
  HA HA HA HA HA Not laughing at your post. Just the munging of the title.  I put in a bug report to the blogs.msdn.com complaining that the HtmlEncoding was messed up, pointing them to the Date&Time munging as an example. Then they sent me an email back that they'd fixed it, and sure enough, your Date&Time title was fixed - it wasn't Date&amp;Time anymore.  It appears they just hacked up a Replace(overlyescapedtitle, "&amp;", "&") line instead of finding and removing the extra HtmlEncode.  ROFL  I laugh so I will not cry...
  Just wondering about a little detail here: "For MS-DOS 2.0, the designers of DOS chose a hybrid version - they already had support for drive letters from DOS 1.0, so they needed to continue using that."  Where did the drive letters come from, then? I really hate them :) Happily I can, and do, use junctions points now, but you still need to have at least one left. It's so much cleaner with the unified filesystem :) And it is easier to discover special files like the CON, NUL, LPT devices when you can just do dir /devices or something like that.
  Drive letters came about (at least to my understanding) because the first PCs supported two disk drives.   At the time assigning them letters solved the problem of how to access them. A: for the first drive, B: for the second. When the Hard drive came out it was given drive letter C: because it was the next in line.
  I don't understand why the switch/parameter character cannot be present in paths. Unix has no problems working with paths that have - in them, most systems, including Windows, can handle paths with spaces in them even though spaces are used to separate arguments and so on.
    > Where did the drive letters come from, then?    Why CP/M of course, which QDOS (and hence DOS 1) was a clone of. Read the first chapter of  http://www.iso.port.ac.uk/~mike/interests/chistory/documents/cpm-22-manual/ here to see where DOS's real heritage lies...
  > How do you remove the file named "-r"?   With "rm -- -r" of course. There's actually quite a few ways to do this.
    I wish it was possible to have a directory junction pointing at a drive letter for a mounted UNC path. That's the only thing that escapes my amazing single filesystem: I have some stuff that lives on another computer, and there's no way to map L:\My Documents to C:\My Documents were L:\ is a mounted path. It works fine otherwise.  Why is this the case anyway? Why are mounted UNC paths any different?
  I'm way too young know this first-hand, so I'll ask.  Why is there a "\" key on the keyboard in the first place? As far as I know, they aren't used in English grammar, and to this day, most common users don't know which is "back" and which is "forward."  I suppose I can ask the same about "{" and "}", but I don't mind those as much.  Since DOS and Windows handle "/" as well as "\", it'd be nice if it would switch to using "/" by default, since Internet URLs use forward slashes, and most people are familiar with them (even though browsers/web servers have to support "\" as well, because of the problem I mentioned earlier, which ultimately lead to security bugs in server-side web software).  I can't imagine switching to "/" would hurt much backwards compatability, since most path paramters have to be put in quotes these days (thanks to the needlessly long "Document and Settings" and "Program Files").  I too would like to get rid of drive letters. I'm consdering mounting my all-in-one memory-card reader onto a folder on my hard drive because I'm tired of having C:, D:, E:, F:, G:, H:, I:, J: all show up under My Computer (and X:, Y:, and Z:, but that was my doing). If I mount everything, I can pretend that C: stands for "Computer," but I suspect mounting CD-ROMs causes problems for some pesky copy-protected games (which are another pet peeve of mine).  I was hoping Monad would help reduce the visibility of drive letters, but I was wrong. It doesn't even support changing the current path to a UNC share, which is a HUGE limitation of both CMD.EXE and any future shells.
  > Why is there a "\" key on the keyboard in the first place? As far as I know, they aren't used in English grammar, and to this day, most common users don't know which is "back" and which is "forward."   The backslash was invented by Bob Bemer  http://www.thocp.net/biographies/bemer_bob.htm  It's use was so you could make the /\ and \/ symbols (AND and OR if you've ever done boolean logic) in the ALGOL language.
  And of course I meant "its" not "it's". As is oft requested, I wish the msdn blogs had a preview button.
  > And where did CPM get them from? The DEC operating systems I mentioned above.  I thought the question was about drive letters specifically.  An RSX-11 file path looks like DR0:[30,12]FILE.TXT;10  A CP/M file path for a file on the first floppy looks like A:PROGRAM.COM  Tim Patterson who wrote QDOS (the basis for DOS 1) did it based on a CP/M manual. I've never heard it mentioned that he spent any time on any DEC machines.  There's nothing wrong with DOS originally being a clone of CP/M (similar to how Linux started out as a clone of U*IX), I just think people often gloss over the history.  My favorite Gary Kildall quote on the issue: "Ask Bill [Gates] why the string in function 9 is terminated by a dollar sign. Ask him, because he can't answer, only I know that."
title: 为什么DOS路径字符为“ \”？
title_en: Why is the DOS path character “\”?

note:
加密货币不再是等待破裂的泡沫。全球各地的个人和企业已经将虚拟货币作为一种新的支付方式。比特币是最重要的。现在，不同行业的公司允许其客户通过比特币为商品或服务付款。这样的实体包括微软，星巴克，家得宝和宝马。
 这种趋势不仅在西方国家。在非洲，人们绝大多数接受加密货币进行汇款，商业交易，甚至为非政府组织筹款。
 根据总部位于美国的区块链研究公司Chainalysis的数据，到2020年6月，非洲与世界其他地区之间的每月加密货币转移每年增长55％以上，达到3.16亿美元。该数字适用于不超过10,000美元的交易。
 尼日利亚，南非和肯尼亚是非洲的一些国家，这些国家对比特币和其他加密货币的接受程度不断提高。在本文中，我们的重点将放在非洲的小型企业如何使用/可以使用比特币上。
   大多数非洲货币的价值经常在下降。潜在因素之一是投资者信心不足。非洲大陆上的各个国家通常会经历政治动荡，挪用公共资金以及无效的财政政策。这些方面，再加上一国对商品和服务的需求相对较低，是造成非洲货币贬值的原因。
 基于这种现状，非洲小企业可以使用比特币的一种方式是作为价值的存储。例如，如果尼日利亚的一个小企业主在2019年初购买并持有价值10,000奈拉的比特币，那么一年后他的投资将增加约85％。
 另一方面，将10,000奈拉（Niras）存入银行帐户将产生1.25％的利率。如果进一步考虑当前的12.8％通货膨胀率，最好将资金保存为数字货币，而不是不稳定的非洲货币。 
近年来，非洲的小企业开始接受从中国进口的产品。这是由于它们的低价格和可用性。购买的商品从汽车零件到电子产品，机械，家具，时装和文具。
 即使受到持续的冠状病毒大流行对经济的影响，中国对非洲大陆的出口在2020年1月至2020年11月之间也增长了0.6％。该数字与去年同期相比。增加的总额为1014.7亿美元。根据这些数字，非洲的小企业正在不断从中国进口商品。
 作为非洲商人使用比特币的方式的一部分，可以通过数字货币为进口产品付款。首先，加密货币降低了交易成本。在传统的付款方式中，费用是已付款金额的百分比。这意味着，如果您打算将一笔巨款转给您的海外供应商，则扣除的费用将是可观的。相比之下，发送比特币的费用要少一些。借助比特币，非洲的商人还能够绕过银行转账限制并克服外汇有限的流动性。
 此外，这种加密货币还可以保护非洲的小型企业免受币种不稳定的交易成本的影响。如前所述，非洲货币相对于最重要的美元而言往往不稳定。
 此外，根据付款方式的不同，向国外转账现金通常需要大约1-2个工作日。幸运的是，比特币转移可以在几分钟之内完成。对于非洲的小企业而言，快速的交易可以消除与付款相关的商品延迟。此外，在尼日利亚等一些非洲国家/地区，出境汇款受到限制，通常比许多企业从国外订购商品所需的金额要少得多。
  正确的营销涉及使用多方面的方法。在当今世界，便利性是促进商品或服务推广的因素之一。在非洲的小型企业中，方便人们付款的产品将吸引更多的客户。比特币很容易完成这项任务。
